source_pr.title: planner: Stabilise TestPartitionWithVariedDataSources
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: close #36740

Problem Summary:
Checking if a query was served from the plan cache or not may be unstable, when doing it 100s of times.
So allow a small fraction to not be cached, when the vast majority is.

### What is changed and how it works?

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [ ] Unit test
- [x] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No code

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note
None
```

root_cause_explanation: The test repeatedly checks `@@last_plan_from_cache` across many randomized loops and previously asserted it always matched the expected cached/non-cached state. In practice, plan cache behavior can be unstable over many iterations due to background changes and cache behavior, so the strict assertion can fail intermittently. This PR stabilizes the test by (1) collecting cache-miss occurrences and allowing a small fraction of non-cached queries, and (2) adding `ANALYZE TABLE` to reduce variability.
fix_pattern: Avoid assuming 100% plan-cache hits across many loops; allow a small miss rate and ensure ANALYZE/stats so plan-cache behavior is less variable.
